text:
Fortunately or no, drought elimination in the western U.S. via tropical storm is unlikely.  OTOH, in the eastern U.S., I remember a drought people were saying would take decades or longer to eliminate.  Then we had 2 hurricanes.  "Yep, we're good on water now!"  

The 2 genders: drought & flood.

images.alt: A map showing the Eastern and Central North Pacific from 180W to 80W. Large white text over the ocean states: "Tropical cyclone activity is not expected during the next 7 days." A box in the top right corner says "No Disturbances." The map shows the west coast of North and Central America and the Hawaiian Islands. At the bottom left, the timestamp is 04:16 AM PDT Fri May 15 2026. A legend at the bottom defines color-coded areas for cyclone formation chances: yellow (< 40%), orange (40-60%), and red (> 60%), along with symbols for depressions, storms, and hurricanes. No colored areas or symbols are present on the current map.

Text-based bulletin from the NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL issued at 5:00 AM PDT Fri May 15 2026. For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude: "Tropical cyclone formation is not expected during the next 7 days." It notes today is the first day of the hurricane season (ending Nov 30). Long-term averages are 15 named storms, 8 hurricanes, and 4 major hurricanes.

Includes the full list of names and pronunciations. It notes issuance times are 5 AM, 11 AM, 5 PM, and 11 PM PDT (shifting to 4 AM, 10 AM, 4 PM, and 10 PM PST in November).

value.text:

text:
Is a coastal city at 'point of no return'? | Letters to the editor
->South Florida Sun Sentinel | More on "New Orleans climate change flooding" at BigEarthData.ai | #Coastal #Ocean

author.displayName: Climate, Ecology, War & More - Dr Glen Barry BigEarthData.ai

external.description: I just want a livable future for myself and my generation. A new study has found that the city of New Orleans has reached a “point of no return” on climate change. The researchers conclude that the city could be underwater by the end of this century, and it’s not an exaggeration to say that cities and towns on the Florida coast could suffer a similar fate. Southern Louisiana is plagued by coastal erosion, rising sea levels, and stronger and stronger hurricanes. These issues have been exacerbated by the operations of oil and gas companies along the coastline, and efforts to hold them accountable for these impacts have been met with staunch opposition from state leadership and the industry. The study is in the April issue of the journal Nature Sustainability. According to the researchers, even if we stopped global warming today, New Orleans is beyond saving and that relocation of residents should begin immediately. They recommend that federal, state and local leaders take a proactive approach to relocation and support infrastructure build-out in safer areas further north. — Elsy Shallman, Loxahatchee Recklessness in Hollywood Hollywood residents should be deeply concerned about the new efforts to turn public oceanfront land...
external.title: Is a coastal city at 'point of no return'? | Letters to the editor

text:
Wetlands act as natural sponges that absorb excess water, slowing down runoff and reducing the volume of water that reaches rivers and streams. This process mitigates the risk of flash floods and protects surrounding properties from flood damage.

text:
As #Virginia experiences growing #flood losses bc of #climatechange, the brunt will be felt in the Southwest of the state, along with Chesapeake Bay & Norfolk. Yet Southwest Virginia lacks key #weather data. What are cost-savvy solutions?
www.wvtf.org/news/2026-05...

images.alt:
Headline on Radio IQ NPR story: Weather data inequity in a flash flood hotspot

Intro: July 18th was a sunny summer day in Russell County in Southwest Virginia -- until a thunderstorm cropped up over the small former coal town of Dante. No one thought much of it. Afternoon storms are common in July...

Photo of a white woman with sandy hair pulled back in a bun standing in front of a small vinyl sided home. 
Caption: Tina Sanders, 50, and her boyfriend were riding in a truck last July (2025) when their road, Sawmill Hollow, was suddenly underwater during flash flooding. Sanders was pulled under a tunnel, along Sawmill Hollow, down Lick Creek where she hit her head and the strong waters dragged her down through the town of Dante. She said it's a miracle she survived.

#NWS #flood #nwsflashflood #FlashFloodWarning Flash Flood Warning for Brooks, GA; Thomas, GA #GAwx
At 1253 AM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 5 and 9 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Pavo, Barwick and Hollis.

#NWS #flood #nwsflashflood #FlashFloodWarning Flash Flood Warning for Brooks, GA; Thomas, GA #GAwx
FFWTAE

The National Weather Service in Tallahassee has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northwestern Brooks County in south central Georgia...
East Central Thomas County in south central Georgia...

* Until 300 AM EDT.

* At 1153 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 5 and 9 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Pavo, Barwick and Hollis.

area. Between 5 and 7 inches of rain have fallen. Additional
rainfall amounts of 1 to 3 inches are possible in the warned area.

Creek.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
#NWS #flood #nwsflashflood #FlashFloodWarning Flash Flood Warning for Brooks, GA; Cook, GA #GAwx
FFWTAE

The National Weather Service in Tallahassee has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
North Central Brooks County in south central Georgia...
Southwestern Cook County in south central Georgia...

* Until 115 AM EDT.

* At 1052 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 3 and 5 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 3 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban
areas, highways, streets and underpasses as well as
other poor drainage and low-lying areas.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Adel, Sparks, Greggs, Pine Valley, Cook Co A/P and Barney.

text: Waymo has issued a voluntary recall for 3791 robotaxis following an incident in San Antonio where an autonomous vehicle drove into a flooded roadway and was subsequently swept into a creek. The company filed a notice with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration after the April 20 incident

text: Flood Alert for Humber estuary from Spurn Point to Winestead Outfall

external.description:
High tides today may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible from 5:30pm to 5:45pm on 15th May 2026. 
Locations most affected will be low lying land and roads near rivers or on the coast. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 8:00PM on 15 May 2026 or as the situation changes.
external.title: Flood Alert for Humber estuary from Spurn Point to Winestead Outfall

text: Flood Alert for Tyne and Wear coast

external.description:
High tides this afternoon may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible from 1:00PM to 5:00PM on 15/02/2026. 
Strong winds combined with high tides may lead to wave overtopping. Locations most affected will be coastal roads and footpaths in Hendon Promenade, Seaburn Lower Promenade and Seaham Harbour and Docks between 1:00PM and 5:00PM. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Avoid coastal roads, footpaths and carparks, which may be flooded . 
This message will be updated by 7:00PM on 15 May 2026 or as the situation changes.
external.title: Flood Alert for Tyne and Wear coast

text: Flood Alert for Tidal Thames riverside from Putney Bridge to Teddington Weir

external.description:
High tides today may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible from 1:15 PM to 3:15 PM on 15/05/2026. 
The forecast high tide at Richmond is 4.60 mAOD at 2:15 PM on 15/05/2026.

We believe there is a possibility of flooding for Putney Embankment (SW15), Chiswick Mall and Strand on the Green (W4), Thames Bank at Mortlake (SW14), Ranelagh Drive (TW1), Friars Lane and Water Lane (TW9), Riverside and The Embankment at Twickenham, and the Towpath below Teddington Lock. Flooding of properties is not expected.

Astronomical tide levels are rising. The river flow at Teddington Weir is 22 cubic metres per second and is steady. We anticipate the flows to remain steady for the next few days. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 8:15PM on 15 May 2026 or as the situation changes.
external.title: Flood Alert for Tidal Thames riverside from Putney Bridge to Teddington Weir

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 6:32PM AKDT until May 15 at 9:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam occurring.

* WHERE...Napaskiak, Oscarville, And Napakiak.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 615 PM AKDT, the River Watch Team reported an ice jam on
the Kuskokwim River near Napaskiak, Oscarville, and Napakiak
remains intact and continues to cause localized flooding of
roads, boardwalks, and low-lying areas within these
communities. Impounding water due to the ice jam will
continue to cause river rises upstream in these communities.

- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along the Kuskokwim River stay alert and be
prepared to take action.

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 1:26PM AKDT until May 15 at 7:30PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by snowmelt is expected to continue near
Stevens Village.

* WHERE...Yukon River near Stevens Village.

* WHEN...Until 730 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Flooding of rivers, creeks, streams, and other low-lying
and flood-prone locations is imminent or occurring.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 121 PM AKDT, Rapid snowmelt is causing continued high
water flows along the Yukon River. Rapid river rises have
already caused flooding near Stevens Village. As of 1PM
Thursday, water was reported over the bank and coming into
town. The barge landing was completely underwater.
- http://www.weather.gov/aprfc

- If you live near or along the Yukon River, stay alert and be
prepared to take action.

text:
Flood Advisory issued May 13 at 9:57PM AKDT until May 14 at 3:45PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam continues.

* WHERE...Bethel, Kwethluk

* WHEN...Until 345 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
This includes Kwethluk as well as low-lying areas in Bethel
including near Brown Slough and Alligator Acres. River or stream
flows are elevated.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 830 PM AKDT, the River Watch Team reported water levels
near 9.3 feet in Bethel at the BETA2 river gauge. These
levels should remain steady or slowly rise through tomorrow.
An ice jam remains in place a few miles south of Bethel,
between Napakiak and Oscarville-Napaskiak. Impounding water
due to the ice jam will cause river rises.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along the Kuskokwim Rover at Bethel,
including Brown Slough and Alligator Acres, or Kwethluk stay alert
and be prepared to take action.

text:
Flood Advisory issued May 13 at 9:52PM AKDT until May 14 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is expected.

* WHERE...Tuluksak

* WHEN...Until 400 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
River or stream flows are elevated.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 830 PM AKDT, the River Watch Team reported that the main
ice jam in the channels of the Kuskokwim River at the top of
Undo Island remains in place.  Water levels remain high
around town and will likely remain steady or slowly rise.
Impounding water due to the ice jam is causing water to rise.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along Tuluksak stay alert and be prepared to
take action.

text:
🚨 Flood Watch issued May 12 at 9:42AM AKDT until May 13 at 10:00AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is possible.

* WHERE...Kalskag, Kwethluk, Akiak and Tuluksak

* WHEN...Through Wednesday morning.

* IMPACTS...Ice jams may result in flooding of rivers, creeks,
sloughs, and other low-lying flood prone locations. Water may
impound behind a jam which may cause flooding several miles
upstream.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- For Kalskag: As of Monday morning, the River Watch Team
reports that an ice jam is still in place near Lower Kalsgag.
This is causing flooding along Airport Road around the school
and tank farm. Meanwhile, ice downstream of Kalskag through
to the mouth of Bogus Creek remains relatively healthy and
intact. Because of the ice jam upstream and intact ice
downstream, Kalskag faces a heightened flood threat and water
levels could rise beyond where they are this morning.
Residents of Kalskag should remain alert to changes in ice
and water levels.

For Kwethluk: Ice jammed in the lower Kuskokuak channel at
and just upstream of the Y, or confluence with the Akiak
Channel of the Kuskokwim, has resulted in water rising in and
near Kwethluk. River Watch aerial reconnaissance noted water
has already started inundating surrounding low-level areas
near the community. Aerial reconnaissance also noted that
there are several spots of open water in the upper portion of
the Kuskokuak Channel, as well as downstream beyond the Y.
Until the ice shifts or moves, residents should prepare for
continued rising water levels, particularly in flood-prone
lower areas.

Akiak and Tuluksak: Original ice still in place which may
cause flooding as the breakup front moves downstream from
Kalskag.

- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text: Flood Alert for The tidal River Yare from Thorpe St Andrew to Breydon Water

external.description:
Tide locking, where high tides restrict normal drainage of the river out to sea, today may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible between 6:30pm and 8:30pm tonight, on Monday 11th May 2026. 
We expect to see high water levels in Brundall and Reedham riverside areas. River levels will remain high this evening due to tide locking, which is where tides restrict normal drainage of the river out to the sea. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 10:00AM on 12 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

external.description:
Tide locking, where high tides restrict normal drainage of the river out to sea, this evening may lead to flooding. 
Flooding is possible between 7pm and 9pm tonight on Monday 11th May 2026. 
We expect to see high water levels at Beccles Quay. River levels will remain high this evening due to tide locking, which is where tides restrict normal drainage of the river out to the sea. 
We are closely monitoring the situation . 
Take care and avoid walking, cycling or driving through flood water . 
This message will be updated by 10:00AM on 12 May 2026 or as the situation changes.

text:
Flood Advisory issued May 10 at 7:21PM AKDT until May 11 at 8:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam.

* WHERE...Aniak.

* WHEN...Until 800 PM AKDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
River or stream flows are elevated due to ice jam downstream of
Aniak. Water on roadways in Aniak is possible.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 711 PM AKDT, the River Watch Team reported an ice jam is
still in place on Kuskokwim River downstream of Aniak near
Birch Crossing. Jammed ice continues to remain tightly packed
from the toe of the jam up to the gravel pit at Aniak. Water
levels have decreased over the past 24 hours and this trend
is expected to continue.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along Aniak stay alert and be prepared to
take action in case conditions change.

text:
🚨 Flood Watch issued May 9 at 11:12PM AKDT until May 10 at 8:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is possible.

* WHERE...Kalskag and Kwethluk.

* WHEN...Through Sunday evening.

* IMPACTS...Ice jams may result in flooding of rivers, creeks,
sloughs, and other low-lying flood prone locations. Water may
impound behind a jam which may cause flooding several miles
upstream.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- For Aniak: Please see the Flood Warning in place for Aniak.

For Kalskag: As of Saturday evening, the River Watch Team
reported that ice near Kalskag continues to move and shift.
Meanwhile, ice downstream of Kalskag through to the mouth of
Bogus Creek remains relatively healthy and intact. Residents
of Kalskag should remain alert to changes in ice and water
levels. Ice was observed to have flushed through the top of
Old River, but remains in place and showing signs of
weakening/rotting close to the mouth where Old River joins
back with the main channel Kuskokwim.

For Kwethluk: Ice jammed in the lower Kuskokuak channel at
and just upstream of the Y, or confluence with the Akiak
Channel of the Kuskokwim, has resulted in water rising in and
near Kwethluk. River Watch aerial reconnaissance noted water
has already started inundating surrounding low-level areas
near the community. Aerial reconnaissance also noted that
there are several spots of open water in the upper portion of
the Kuskokuak Channel, as well as downstream beyond the Y.
Until the ice shifts or moves, residents should prepare for
continued rising water levels, particularly in flood-prone
lower areas.

- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text:
🚨 Flood Watch issued May 9 at 8:23PM AKDT until May 10 at 8:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is possible.

* WHERE...Aniak, Kalskag and Kwethluk

* WHEN...Through Sunday evening.

* IMPACTS...Ice jams may result in flooding of rivers, creeks,
sloughs, and other low-lying flood prone locations. Water may
impound behind a jam which may cause flooding several miles
upstream.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- For Aniak: As of Saturday evening, the River Watch Team
reported that ice jamming in both the Kuskokwim River and the
Aniak Slough continues to result in high water levels for
Aniak and the surrounding area. Please see the Flood Warning
in place for Aniak for more details.

For Kalskag: As of Saturday evening, the River Watch Team
reported that ice near Kalskag continues to move and shift.
Meanwhile, ice downstream of Kalskag through to the mouth of
Bogus Creek remains relatively health and intact. Residents
of Kalskag should remain alert to changes in ice and water
levels. Ice was observed to have flushed through the top of
Old River, but remains in place and showing signs of
weakening/rotting close to the mouth where Old River joins
back with the main channel Kuskokwim.

For Kwethluk: Ice jammed in the lowker Kuskokuak channel at
and just upstream of the Y, or confluence with the Akiak
Channel of the Kuskokwim, has resulted in water rising in and
near Kwethluk. River Watch aerial reconnaissance noted water
has already started inundating surrounding low -level areas
near the community. Aerial reconnaissance also noted that
there are several spots of open water in the upper portion of
the Kuskokuak Channel, as well as downstream beyond the Y.
Until the ice shifts or moves, residents should prepare for
continued rising water levels, particularly in flood-prone
lower areas.

- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is possible.

* WHERE...Aniak and Kalskag

* WHEN...Through Sunday evening.

* IMPACTS...Ice jams may result in flooding of rivers, creeks,
sloughs, and other low-lying flood prone locations. Water may
impound behind a jam which may cause flooding several miles
upstream.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- For Aniak: As of 9am Friday May 8th, the Riverwatch Team
reported that the ice jam on the Kuskokwim River at Aniak
started to move downstream and jammed again approximately 16
miles downriver, below Birch Crossing. Additional ice flushed
from upstream has now piled onto the tail of that jam,
causing water levels at Aniak to rise late Thursday night.
Water is overbank near the Aniak Runway, with water over the
back road to the barge landing and the gravel pit. Water is
also near the AC store and homes on the east/upstream side.
Please see the flood warning in effect for Aniak for more
information.

For Kalskag: As of late afternoon Thursday May 7th, the ice
at Kalskag has shifted. Many river-wide cracks, sheet
separations, and pressure ridges have formed from upstream of
Upper Kalskag to near the islands off Lower Kalskag.
Localized break up has occurred near the mouth of the Old
River, but it has not yet flushed.

- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

feet this morning. It will then fall below flood stage late
this afternoon.

🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 10:43PM EDT until May 16 at 3:12AM EDT by NWS Indianapolis IN 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following river and location
in Indiana...

White River at Hazleton.

.Flooding continues on the lower White River near Hazleton, though
river levels are gradually falling. Minor flooding is expected to
continue through late Friday or early Saturday morning.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...White River at Hazleton.

* WHEN...Until early Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...At 17.0 feet, Lowest reading available from the Hazleton
Staff Gage.  Since the 1930s the White River has moved from the
"old wellhouse" to several hundred yards from the "old
wellhouse".  The river returns to the "old wellhouse" around 17 to
17.5 feet.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- There is no current observed data.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
tomorrow afternoon and continue falling and remain below
flood stage.
- Flood stage is 16.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 6:32PM AKDT until May 15 at 9:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam occurring.

* WHERE...Bethel, Kwethluk

* WHEN...Until 900 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
This includes Kwethluk as well as low-lying areas in Bethel
including near Brown Slough and Alligator Acres. River or stream
flows are elevated and flooding of low-lying areas has been
observed.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 615 PM AKDT, the River Watch Team reported water levels
near 10.4 feet in Bethel at the BETA2 river gauge. These
levels will continue to slowly rise through tomorrow with
localized flooding of roads, boardwalks, and low-lying areas
of Bethel and Kwethluk. An ice jam remains in place a few
miles south of Bethel, between Napakiak and Oscarville-
Napaskiak. Impounding water due to the ice jam will cause
river rises.
- For Kuskokwim River at Bethel:
At 5:50pm the stage was 10.4 feet.
Flood Stage is 8.5 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along the Kuskokwim Rover at Bethel,
including Brown Slough and Alligator Acres, or Kwethluk stay alert
and be prepared to take action.

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 6:39PM AKDT until May 15 at 10:00PM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by high water and heavy ice runs continues.

* WHERE...Galena.

* WHEN...Until 1000 PM AKDT Friday.

* IMPACTS...High water may result in flooding of rivers, creeks,
streams, and other low-lying and flood-prone locations.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 630 PM AKDT, the ice jam at Yuki River has released, with
water and ice continuing to move towards and past Galena.
This will lead to higher water levels and potential lowland
flooding.

- The amount of water behind the ice jam that released along
the Yukon River near the mouth of the Yuki River is higher
than 2023 levels.

- Http://www.weather.gov/aprfc

Help Nauiyu families return home safely after flooding by replacing essentials and rebuilding stability following evacuation.

Your support helps residents recover from disrupted daily life.
 https://cryptohumanity.org/causes/nauiyu-daly-river-flood-evacuee-support

external.description: Support Nauiyu, Australia residents recovering after major Daly River flooding forced evacuations and damaged homes and access routes.
external.title: Nauiyu Daly River Flood Evacuee Support | CryptoHumanity

text:
Eid al-Adha is coming. It has been a year since the corrupt Indonesian government turned a blind eye to the flood victims in Aceh and Sumatra. They are now homeless and must spend two Eid in evacuation centers.

If you have some spare money, you can make an international donation through this org:

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 12 at 10:41AM CDT until May 14 at 7:00AM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA 🚨
Additional Details Here.

author.displayName: Alerts from NWS New Orleans LA (UNOFFICIAL)

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in
Louisiana...

Tangipahoa River Near Robert affecting Tangipahoa Parish.

Tchefuncte River Above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington affecting
St. Tammany Parish.

For the Tangipahoa River...including Osyka, Kentwood, Tangi. River
near Tickfaw, Amite, Robert...Moderate flooding is forecast.
For the Tchefuncte River...including Folsom, U.S. Highway 190 Near
Covington...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...Tchefuncte River above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington.

* WHEN...Until Thursday morning.

* IMPACTS...At 22.0 feet, Tchefuncte River camp grounds will be
under a few inches of water. The Goodbee community is threatened
with flooding. Water will invade Camp Tchefuncte's parking lot.
Vehicles must be removed to higher ground.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:30 AM CDT Tuesday the stage was 21.3 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 16.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
early Thursday morning and continue falling to 13.7 feet
Sunday morning.
- Flood stage is 20.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in
Louisiana...

Tangipahoa River Near Robert affecting Tangipahoa Parish.

Tchefuncte River Above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington affecting
St. Tammany Parish.

For the Tangipahoa River...including Osyka, Kentwood, Tangi. River
near Tickfaw, Amite, Robert...Moderate flooding is forecast.
For the Tchefuncte River...including Folsom, U.S. Highway 190 Near
Covington...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHAT...Moderate flooding is occurring and moderate flooding is
forecast.

* WHERE...Tangipahoa River near Robert.

* WHEN...Until late Friday evening.

* IMPACTS...At 18.0 feet, Evacuation of trailers at Hidden Oaks must
be completed. Water covers the entrance to camp grounds. Secondary
roads and unimproved roads north of Robert will flood as
Chappepeela Creek backs up. Low places on Highway 22 south of
Robert will be under water.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:30 AM CDT Tuesday the stage was 18.0 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 8.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
Friday afternoon and continue falling to 14.1 feet Sunday
morning.
- Flood stage is 15.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 10 at 10:12AM CDT until May 14 at 8:00PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA 🚨
Additional Details Here.

author.displayName: Alerts from NWS New Orleans LA (UNOFFICIAL)

images.alt:
...Forecast flooding changed from Minor to Moderate severity and
increased in duration for the following rivers in Louisiana...

Tchefuncte River Above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington affecting
St. Tammany Parish.

For the Tchefuncte River...including Folsom, U.S. Highway 190 Near
Covington...Moderate flooding is forecast.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and moderate flooding is
forecast.

* WHERE...Tchefuncte River above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington.

* WHEN...Until Thursday evening.

* IMPACTS...At 26.0 feet, The entrance road to Camp Tchefuncte will
flood. The campgrounds will be inaccessible by auto and evacuation
of the campground area is advised. Homes between Tantella Ranch
Road and the river as well as homes on Trinchard Road and
Louisiana Highway 1077 will be threatened.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:30 AM CDT Sunday the stage was 24.4 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 16.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise to a crest of 25.0
feet early tomorrow afternoon.
- Flood stage is 20.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

Warning for the following rivers in Louisiana...

Tchefuncte River Near Folsom affecting St. Tammany Parish.

For the Tchefuncte River...including Folsom, U.S. Highway 190 Near
Covington...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...Tchefuncte River near Folsom.

* WHEN...Until tomorrow evening.

* IMPACTS...At 16.0 feet, Areas of Spring Park and Albert Thompson
Road will be inundated. Homes will flood if the river rises
higher. Evacuation of those areas is recommended.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:00 PM CDT Saturday the stage was 15.6 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 15.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise above flood stage
late this evening and continue rising to a crest of 16.5 feet
tomorrow morning. The river is expected to fall below flood
stage early tomorrow afternoon.
- Flood stage is 16.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning is extended for the following rivers in
Louisiana...

Tchefuncte River Above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington affecting
St. Tammany Parish.

For the Tchefuncte River...including Folsom, U.S. Highway 190 Near
Covington...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...Tchefuncte River above U.S. Highway 190 Near Covington.

* WHEN...Until Tuesday evening.

* IMPACTS...At 26.0 feet, The entrance road to Camp Tchefuncte will
flood. The campgrounds will be inaccessible by auto and evacuation
of the campground area is advised. Homes between Tantella Ranch
Road and the river as well as homes on Trinchard Road and
Louisiana Highway 1077 will be threatened.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 7:30 PM CDT Saturday the stage was 22.0 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 16.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise to a crest of 24.0
feet tomorrow morning. The river is expected to fall below
flood stage early Tuesday afternoon.
- Flood stage is 20.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

forecast.

* WHERE...Tangipahoa River near Robert.

* WHEN...Until further notice.

Chappepeela Creek backs up. Low places on Highway 22 south of
Robert will be under water.

- Bankfull stage is 8.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise to a crest of 19.0
feet early Monday afternoon.

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 14 at 11:56AM PDT by NWS Spokane WA 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in
Washington...

Stehekin River at Stehekin affecting Chelan County.

.Warm temperatures will melt high mountain snowpack and increase
river flows.

For the Stehekin River...including Stehekin...Minor flooding is
forecast.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and forecast to continue.

* WHERE...Stehekin River at Stehekin.

* WHEN...Until further notice.

* IMPACTS...At 20.5 feet, water inundates some properties and
overtops the temporary corduroy bridge. Company Creek Road
inundated at northern end. Battalion Creek culverts are beginning
to be overtopped. Water begins damaging Wood Road.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 11:30 AM PDT Thursday the stage was 21.7 feet.
- Bankfull stage is 19.5 feet.
- Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours
ending at 11:30 AM PDT Thursday was 22.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to gradually recede through
the next couple of days, dropping below flood stage by May
17. It is forecasted to rise back above flood stage on May
20.
- Flood stage is 20.5 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text:
As in, they will soon have a 28 ft storm surge in the Netherlands. Same as Katrina.

They are spending ~$2B a year to prepare for this, as the dyke in the picture can only handle about half of that. They plan to be ready for it before it ever arrives.

Even if they're not, they have evacuation plans

text:
They get big storms occasionally, which from the perspective of catastrophic flooding, is the same thing.

They're not worried about wind and rain. It's all storm surge and waves.

Louisiana gets 2x the storm surge height (~15ft vs 28 ft). But Netherlands builds to withstand 1 in 10,000 year storms.

record.text:

value.text:
That was my question!

They Dutch'd me with the answer:

Hurricane doesn't matter. The only thing that matters is storm surge + wave height.

The lowest point in this town is 13 ft below sea level. A 200 year storm gives us 15 ft, so 28 ft above ground level. Then big waves could take us to 54 ft.

text:
Also, please explain the point about how the only thing that matters with hurricanes vs Netherlands storms is the 15 ft vs 28 ft of storm surge, but how 28 ft can be handled too.

If Dutch people had been given all of Louisiana 600 years ago, could they / would they have poldered the whole thing?

record.text:

If Dutch people had been given all of Louisiana 600 years ago, could they / would they have poldered the whole thing?

text: And the trick isn’t the average sea level, it’s the hurricanes. The Netherlands doesn’t regularly seem to have 10 meters of storm surge.

text:
🚨 Flash Flood Warning issued May 12 at 10:52PM EDT until May 13 at 1:15AM EDT by NWS Tallahassee FL 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
FFWTAE

The National Weather Service in Tallahassee has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
North Central Brooks County in south central Georgia...
Southwestern Cook County in south central Georgia...

* Until 115 AM EDT.

* At 1052 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 3 and 5 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 3 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban
areas, highways, streets and underpasses as well as
other poor drainage and low-lying areas.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Adel, Sparks, Greggs, Pine Valley, Cook Co A/P and Barney.

external.description: Faced with flooding and drought, these new kinds of eco-cities offer a way to incorporate the water cycle into town planning.

record.text: David Shukman (former BBC now @granthamlse.bsky.social) does a great job in his new book (The Response @penguinrandomhouse.bsky.social) of both making you very alarmed about the future, but also somehow less helpless. Our latest #LSEFutureProof video with him explores the risks of urban flooding.

external.title: Urban flooding and emergency preparedness: is the UK ready? | LSE Future Proof
external.description: YouTube video by LSE

text:
🚨 Flash Flood Warning issued May 11 at 9:35PM CDT until May 11 at 11:00PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA 🚨
Additional Details Here.

author.displayName: Alerts from NWS New Orleans LA (UNOFFICIAL)

images.alt:
FFWLIX

The National Weather Service in New Orleans has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northwestern St. Charles Parish in southeastern Louisiana...
Central St. John The Baptist Parish in southeastern Louisiana...

* Until 1100 PM CDT.

* At 935 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 2 and 4 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban
areas, highways, streets and underpasses as well as
other poor drainage and low-lying areas.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Laplace, Reserve, Hahnville, Killona, Montz, Edgard, Taft, Norco,
Garyville, New Sarpy, Wallace and Destrehan.

This includes the following Interstate...
Interstate 10 in Louisiana between mile markers 203 and 210.

text:
🚨 Flash Flood Warning issued May 11 at 8:32PM CDT until May 11 at 9:00PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA 🚨
Additional Details Here.

author.displayName: Alerts from NWS New Orleans LA (UNOFFICIAL)

images.alt:
At 832 PM CDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain across the warned area. Between 3 and 6 inches of rain have
fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 2 inches in 1 hour.
Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 3 inches are possible in the
warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Baton Rouge, Denham Springs, Oak Hills Place, St. Gabriel, Village
St. George, Old Jefferson, Inniswold, Shenandoah, Westminster,
Gardere and Prairieville.

This includes the following Interstates...
Interstate 10 in Louisiana between mile markers 156 and 173.
Interstate 12 between mile markers 1 and 9.
Interstate 110 in Louisiana near mile marker 2.

Excellente initiative !
A quand l'extension aux parkings relais, à ceux des hôpitaux ou cliniques...
Avec des bornes de recharge sur chaque pilier ?
Dommage que sur cette photo, le sol soit bitumé. Des dalles alvéolées seraient plus pertinentes, non ?
Absorption d'eau, risque inondation réduit

value.text:
France is mandating solar panels on every large commercial car park in the country — turning millions of square metres of idle asphalt into one of Europe's most productive clean energy assets.
I have been mentioning this for months - they are shooting up on all the supermarket car parks.

En vrai, le premier point qui me rassure beaucoup par rapport au Covid-19 : 

- Le foyer d'infection n'est pas en Chine et on l'a localisé assez tôt.

Parce que le temps que le parti communiste chinois admettait à Wuhan que c'était la giga merde, le Covid-19 était devenu une pandémie.

🔴 Emmanuel Macron affirme que le gouvernement a pris «les bonnes décisions» sur l'hantavirus et que «la situation est sous contrôle».

Notre DIRECT ➡️ https://l.leparisien.fr/YuDS

text: Vagues de chaleur, incendies, pluies torrentielles : pourquoi 2026 s’annonce extrême

external.description: Braises d’un ton. Les premiers mois de l’année ont été marqués par une intensification spectaculaire des évènements extrêmes. Avec le retour du phénomène El Niño, 2026 promet d'être hors normes, ont alerté mardi des climatologues du monde entier.

text: [3/8] En Espagne, la tension vient d’un fort contraste géographique. Les besoins en eau restent élevés toute l’année, mais le climat alterne sécheresses longues et pluies torrentielles, surtout dans les régions arides et semi-arides de la péninsule Ibérique.

text:
To Marc By Kim Rhee young

ولدت الشاعرة كيم ري يونغ في سيول ودرست الرقص. debut عام 1991 بجائزة قصيدتها "حزن كلب ميت". درست الفن في الولايات المتحدة وكتبت نصوص رقص. تمتلك عدة مجموعات شعرية وحصلت على جوائز أدبية. أصبحت أيضًا عاملة اجتماعية في حكومات محلية. تعكس قصائدها مشاعر الحب والتعلق.
